rulings.Case Title: THOMAS DUFFY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / D. Zelinsky And Sons, Inc.S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 15, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the drywall mud to which defendant's employees exposed him contained asbestos. Mr. Ay's declaration demonstrates that not all joint compounds during the time contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Charles Ay's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 134-135, as well as to all the statements throughout Mr. Ay's declaration opining that joint compounds contained asbestos through the mid-to-late 1970s, contained in paragraphs 109, 111, 112, 116, 118, 119, 129-132. Defendant's other objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS DUFFY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 15, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Balliet Bros. Construction Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 2.7(B) and 2.11(T) for defendant's failure to provide courtesy copies. Defendant failed to lodge with the Court a courtesy copy of its reply prior to the initially-scheduled March 9, 2017 hearing date and defendant subsequently failed to comply with the Court's order, issued in its March 8, 2017 tentative ruling and entered in the minutes, to lodge such courtesy copy with Department 503 no later than March 9, 2017 at 4:00p.m. The Court never received a courtesy copy. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
